resources.string: A responsive personal portfolio built with Compose Multiplatform, featuring dynamic content and smooth animations.
Cross-platform task management app with offline support and sync.
Lightweight API gateway with authentication and rate limiting.
Writing software daily, for fun and growth

For work, I build what’s needed and aim to deliver the best value I can. I was trained in Android development when the timing was right, and over the years I’ve grown comfortable working across modern languages and frameworks. My strength lies in taking abstract ideas — the vision others have for their projects — and translating them into something real. I focus on refining the connections, making sure nothing is missed, and producing results that are solid and maintainable.

I’ve spent my career at JPMorgan Chase. My work centers on front-end architecture: service layers, view models, MVI interfaces, use cases, Composables, fragments, and dependency management. I take pride in building the pieces that make complex systems work smoothly on the client side.

As lead Android developer for my scrum team, I guide two to three junior developers while managing a full workload of my own. I’m known for being process-driven and detail-oriented, making sure every stage of development — from planning through testing — runs cleanly and efficiently.

My career has been a steady progression marked by consistent learning and refinement. I’m focused on continuing to grow, improving both the quality of what I build and the way I lead others in building it.

I think a lot about how fleeting life is and how limited our time can feel, especially in the years when we’re strongest. What keeps me moving are the people I know who are also carrying their own weight but still manage to accomplish what they set out to do. They remind me that it’s possible.

Most of what I do — whether in lifting, personal projects, or life — comes down to faith that progress is possible if I keep at it. I don’t expect huge leaps, just steady improvement over time. Slowing down or stopping tends to make things worse, but going at a reasonable pace keeps me grounded.

When I let go of worry and realize there’s nothing urgent pressing down, I find peace in small moments — petting my animals, meditating, or just being still for a bit. I don’t expect empathy or admiration; I just want to offer a small window into who I am beyond the work. I try to keep moving forward with what I have, one step at a time.
Paint your own masterpiece with Angus Paint

Use your finger as your painting tool. Paint with any color of the rainbow. Paint with different brush sizes. Save your paintings to your device. Paint upon a picture from your device. Paint in portrait or landscape mode. Have fun, and please rate on Google Play!
A new version of Solitaire invented by Angus Software
The objective of the game is to score as many points as possible before the deck runs out of cards.
Read at an enhanced speed with Blink Reader

Blink Reader is an Application for the Android mobile Operating System. The purpose of the Blink Reader app is to provide its users with an Application for reading text at an enhanced speed. This enhanced reading speed is achieved by displaying one word of the text which the user is reading at a time. By displaying one word at a time, the user doesn't have to move their eyes, and can manually control the words per minute at which the text displays.
Improve your touching and pressing aim with this targeting game
With four different modes to help improve you accuracy and speed, Tap Target Booster is fun way to measure and hone your touch screen skills!
Brightness controller for external and internal displays

A brightness controller for external (DDC/CI) and internal (backlight) displays, written in Rust with GTK4/Libadwaita. Features per-display sliders, sync modes, scheduling, and 16 color themes. Available on crates.io and GitHub.
There are no new Posts to display.
This is a dark theme — it may look wrong in light mode
This is a light theme — it may look wrong in dark mode
